inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 common-unit cash distributions paid $149.435M / ~93.396M units ≈ $1.60/unit (10-K_20260226)
inputs.book_source: FY2025 partners' capital ~$1.01B / 93.4M units [approx.; GAAP not fair-value floor]
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence uses declared cash distributions to common unitholders, not GAAP net income ($0.62/unit FY2025). Q1 2026 revenue and net income per unit fell year over year; base uses trailing FY2025 distribution run-rate.

bear.notes: Commodity trough; credit facility amortization absorbs cash; distribution cuts

base.notes: Mid-cycle distributions; bolt-on mineral/ORRI acquisitions offset base decline

bull.notes: Permian activity + accretive deals; undeveloped acreage conversion

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Mineral/ORRI interests on balance sheet at cost; reserve PV in Ryder Scott report exceeds carrying value in downturns [Assumption]

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: ~127,832 gross (9,760 net) undeveloped mineral acres + ~18,554 gross (92 net) undeveloped ORRI acres (10-K_20260226 asset tables)

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: No litigation recovery curve; Q1 2026 weakness is commodity/activity, not contractual suspension

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Producing royalty cash in revenues; growth via acquisitions in MD&A

gaap_vs_fair_value.note: Royalties carried on balance sheet; fair value tied to commodity cash flows and reserve report, not separate NAV mark each quarter

segments_or_options.label: Undeveloped mineral acreage
segments_or_options.notes: Zero in Lawrence base; partial PV in overlay sensitivity only

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Per-acre fair value comps for undeveloped net acres not in Lawrence FCF path

optionality_gate.notes: FY2025 distribution yield ~11.9% at placeholder price; Lawrence IRR is stance gate unless human overrides

growth_explanation.mechanism: Base growth assumes modest distribution increases from bolt-on mineral and ORRI acquisitions (2024-2025 8-K deal flow) offsetting natural decline on legacy properties.
growth_explanation.bear_trigger: WTI/Henry Hub sustained down-cycle cuts distributions below $1.30/unit for four quarters while revolver balance stays above $400M.
growth_explanation.bull_trigger: Distribution run-rate above $1.90/unit sustained with accretive unit/equity-funded acquisitions and Permian rig count recovery.
